full_content:
Pre-Implementation Proof: Requirements Collection Hook (validated against DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION, GOV-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001, DCL-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-CONTRACT-001)

## Work Item Identity

WI ID:               (to be created in MemBase work_items table at bridge filing time)
Slice / Project:     New work — separate from any existing slice
Implementation home: GT-KB framework (the hook is GT-KB platform infrastructure). Per GOV-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-001 RULE 5: hook script lives at `.claude/hooks/requirements-collector.py` within the GT-KB project root.

## Cross-Cutting Constraint Review (per GOV-20 Phase 1 IPR contract)

The implementation MUST satisfy ALL of the following constraints, mapped from the source DELIB and downstream GOV/DCL:

| Constraint | Source | How the implementation satisfies it |
|---|---|---|
| LLM classification per message | DELIB #3 + GOV #4 (CLASSIFICATION section) | Hook calls Haiku-class model on each owner message; classifier returns one of {requirement_candidate, clarification, decision, chat}. |
| 3-tier retrieval (DA + chat + LO advisories) | DELIB #3 + GOV #4 (STEP 2) | Implementation queries: (a) `KnowledgeDB.search_deliberations()` for DA semantic search; (b) `transcript_path` from hook input for last 50 turns; (c) glob+text-similarity over `independent-progress-assessments/CODEX-INSIGHT-DROPBOX/INSIGHTS-*.md`. |
| Exactly 3 clarification options when requirement_candidate | GOV #4 (STEP 2) | LLM call constructs response with options labeled A/B/C; output validation rejects responses with != 3 options. |
| AskUserQuestion presentation | GOV #4 (STEP 3) | Hook output instructs agent to use AskUserQuestion; agent has direct access to that tool. |
| DELIB capture on owner selection | GOV #4 (STEP 4) | Agent captures owner selection via the existing formal-artifact-approval-gate flow, generating a packet with the selected option as the canonical interpretation. |
| Escape hatch for unambiguous requirements | GOV #4 (ESCAPE HATCH) | Hook output includes `behavior_recommendation` field that flags unambiguous cases; agent presents single interpretation when flagged. |
| Hook script at canonical path | DCL #5 (LOCATION) | Implementation places file at `.claude/hooks/requirements-collector.py` per the DCL. |
| Hook registered in `.claude/settings.json` UserPromptSubmit | DCL #5 (LOCATION) | Implementation appends hook entry to `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse → UserPromptSubmit array. |
| Codex parity in `.codex/hooks.json` | DCL #5 (LOCATION) | Implementation adds matching entry to `.codex/hooks.json` (executable when Codex Windows hook parity becomes live). |
| I/O schema conformance | DCL #5 (I/O SCHEMA) | Hook reads stdin per Claude Code UserPromptSubmit contract; emits stdout matching `additionalContext` schema. |
| `additionalContext` schema sections in order | DCL #5 (ADDITIONAL_CONTEXT SCHEMA) | Hook output template enforces section order; integration test validates schema match. |
| Classifier model bounds | DCL #5 (CLASSIFIER MODEL BOUNDS) | Implementation pins model to `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`; tracks per-call token use; enforces 5s timeout with fallback. |
| 10s retrieval timeout total | DCL #5 (RETRIEVAL INTERFACE) | Implementation uses asyncio.wait_for() with 10s budget across all 3 retrievals; partial results allowed with warning. |
| Regression test at canonical path | DCL #5 (TEST COVERAGE) | Implementation creates `tests/scripts/test_requirements_collection_hook.py` (or post-migration equivalent) covering the 5 acceptance criteria. |
| Doctor invariants | DCL #5 (DOCTOR INVARIANTS) | Implementation extends `groundtruth-kb/src/groundtruth_kb/project/doctor.py` with 4 new invariant checks (script existence, registration, model allowlist, test file existence). |
| Capture-event surfacing per DELIB #1 | GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001 | Hook output instructs agent to surface every classification (positive AND negative path) per DELIB #1's transparency rule. |
| Cross-cutting registry registration | GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 | Once the registry is implemented, GOV #4 will be added as a registered cross-cutting requirement; this hook itself becomes one of the rules the registry enforces compliance with. |

## Implementation Build Sequence (proposed)

1. **Stub `scripts/check_project_root_boundary.py`** (also satisfies CCR-PROJECT-ROOT-BOUNDARY-001 fast_check) — even though it's a different cross-cutting requirement, it's a smaller scope dependency that should land first.
2. **Create `.claude/hooks/requirements-collector.py`** — minimal version: classifier-only, no retrieval, returns `additionalContext` with classification + verbatim quote.
3. **Add classifier integration** — Anthropic SDK call to claude-haiku with the classification prompt; cost tracking; timeout handling.
4. **Add 3-tier retrieval** — DA search via `KnowledgeDB.search_deliberations()`; chat retrieval from transcript_path; LO advisory glob+similarity.
5. **Add option generation** — LLM call to construct 3 options from retrieval results.
6. **Add `additionalContext` template** — markdown formatter producing the schema-conformant output.
7. **Register in `.claude/settings.json`** + `.codex/hooks.json`.
8. **Write regression test** at `tests/scripts/test_requirements_collection_hook.py`.
9. **Extend doctor with 4 invariant checks**.
10. **End-to-end smoke**: send a fixture requirement message through the hook; verify classification + 3 options + AskUserQuestion-ready output; verify capture-event log.

## Risks / Rollback

- **Risk**: LLM classifier latency overruns 5s timeout under load. **Mitigation**: timeout fallback to classification == "chat" so user input is never blocked; warning logged for owner visibility.
- **Risk**: 3 generated options are too similar (not mutually exclusive). **Mitigation**: prompt engineering + LLM-side judge call to reject low-divergence options.
- **Risk**: Retrieval latency dominates (DA query slow on cold cache). **Mitigation**: retrieval budget capped; partial results emitted; doctor warns on slow retrievals.
- **Rollback**: deregister hook from `.claude/settings.json` + `.codex/hooks.json`; the hook script can stay on disk inert.

## Verification (post-implementation; future CVR)

A CVR (Constraint Verification Record) will be filed AFTER implementation, demonstrating each constraint above is satisfied with concrete evidence (file paths, test pass logs, doctor output, end-to-end smoke results).

## Bridge Proposal Forecast

Implementation will be filed as a separate bridge thread:
`bridge/gtkb-requirements-collection-hook-implementation-001.md`
under the standard NEW → Codex review → GO → implementation → REPORT → VERIFIED cycle.

This IPR is a pre-implementation reviewability proof, not an implementation proposal itself. It documents that the implementation has been reviewed against all applicable constraints BEFORE the bridge proposal is filed.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
